Metadata.title: Cordée.IA — AI Strategy Advisory for Decision-Makers
Metadata.description: Independent advisory firm. Applied AI for CTOs, COOs, and investment committees. Five phases. One rope.
Metadata.ogDescription: AI advisory for decision-makers. Five phases. One rope.

Hero.subtitle: We help CTOs and industrial leaders turn generative AI into operational leverage. No hype. No pilots that never ship. One method, five phases, measurable outcomes.

Method.intro: What we actually do once you hire us. Not a theoretical framework — a climbing routine refined across 32 engagements. Each phase ends with a signed deliverable.

phases.title: Reconnaissance
phases.desc: Rapid audit of your AI landscape: use cases, data, teams, regulatory constraints. We separate reachable summits from false hopes.

phases.title: Roping up
phases.desc: Workshop with leadership and the tech team. We define roles, guardrails, and rhythm. No ascent succeeds without clear roping.

phases.title: Acclimatisation
phases.desc: One focused use case, shipped in six weeks. RAG, agents, fine-tuning — we pick the right rope for the right wall. No toy demos.

phases.title: Ascent
phases.desc: Scaling up: MLOps, observability, governance. The team climbs steadily, no jolts. You won't need us for the next phase.

phases.title: Summit
phases.desc: We descend. You keep the team, the tools, the expertise. ROI report, capitalisation plan, possible next routes.

Expertise.intro: We only work on what we have operated ourselves. No "discovery" missions on your dime, no slides recycled from another client. If it's not on this list, we'll point you elsewhere.

items.d: Diagnostic, use cases, business case. Decide what to launch, what to stop, what to wait on.
Hybrid RAG, agents, fine-tuning. The right rope for the right wall — no imposed stack.
MLOps, observability, business guardrails. Get out of proofs-of-concept that never ship.
AI Act, GDPR, internal charter. Robust decision framework for investment committees.
Skill-up of tech and business teams. You keep the rope at the end.
Evaluation of products or acquisition targets. What actually works vs. what merely demos.

items.title: You're a CTO
items.quote: "We have 14 POCs. None in production. The ExCom is getting impatient."
points: Map what actually deserves to ship to production
Robust target architecture, not a patchwork of demos

items.title: You're a COO
points: Business guardrails baked in from the pilot
Impact measurement on the ground, not in the lab

items.title: You're ExCom / Board
items.quote: "We fund — but we want to understand what we're signing."
points: Technical due diligence on targets or products
Decision framework aligned with the AI Act

Process.intro: Each milestone is a signed deliverable. No "interim report" gathering dust. By day 47, you have a system in production. By day 180, you no longer need us.

deliverables.d: Use-case map, value × risk matrix, recommendations.
AI charter, steering committee, five priorities chosen.
First use case shipped, measured, transferred.
Documented ROI, autonomous teams, refreshed roadmap.

items.title: Start a project from scratch
items.desc: Answer 6 questions, download the .zip, run claude. 2 minutes instead of 2 hours of CLAUDE.md config.

items.title: Add Claude to an existing project
items.desc: Overlay mode: install.sh detects your stack (Next, Nest, FastAPI, Go, Rust), backs up your existing .claude/, and drops the config in without breaking anything.

items.title: Run a workshop or a course
items.desc: Generate an identical starter for 30 students. Hand out the .zip via Moodle or a shared drive. Everyone starts on equal footing.

items.title: Demo Claude Design in a talk
items.desc: DESIGN.md ready to upload to claude.ai/design. Handoff to Claude Code button. /design-handoff skill scaffolds Next.js. Full chain in 5 minutes.

items.title: Standardise a team
items.desc: Generate your team's official starter once, push it to a GitHub template repo. Every new project inherits it automatically.

items.title: Quick onboarding on a legacy codebase
items.desc: Drop the overlay, run /audit. Claude maps the codebase, lists 5 risky zones, 5 safe zones to start with, suggests 3 safe PRs.

Faq.intro: Six questions that come up in every scoping meeting. If yours isn't here, write to us — we reply within 24h.

items.q: How are you different from a generalist consulting firm?
items.a: We only do applied AI — and only on terrain we operate ourselves. No €1,200/day junior who learns your business by reading trade press. Three senior consultants maximum, on-site every day of the engagement.

items.q: Why a fixed fee instead of T&M?
items.a: Fixed fee commits us to outcomes, not hours. If the engagement overruns, that's our problem, not yours. Consequence: we don't take every subject, and we turn down poorly scoped engagements.

items.q: Which models / vendors do you work with?
items.a: Independent. Depending on the case: Claude, GPT, Mistral, self-hosted Llama. We have no commercial partnership with any model vendor. Recommendations follow business value, not kickbacks.

items.q: What about compliance — AI Act, GDPR, trade secrets?
items.a: Built in from the scoping phase. We work regularly with in-house legal teams and specialised law firms. Sovereign hosting available (OVHcloud, Scaleway) on request.

items.q: How many engagements do you run in parallel?
items.a: Three, never more. That's how we guarantee on-site presence and the fixed fee. Kickoff typically takes 3 weeks after signature — sometimes more if we're booked.

items.q: Do you work with SMBs or only large accounts?
items.a: Both, as long as the subject can be scoped and decisions can be made quickly. We've worked with family-owned mid-caps and SBF 120 groups alike. Minimum fee is €85k (excl. VAT).

CTA.intro: First scoped call — your context, your constraints, what we can actually bring. No slides, no commitment.

Footer.tagline: Independent advisory firm. Applied AI for CTOs, COOs, and investment committees.

Hero.title: Bootstrap a Claude Code + Claude Design project in 2 minutes
Hero.subtitle: Answer 6 questions. Download a ready-to-use .zip. Free, open source, MIT.

HowItWorks.title: How it works
HowItWorks.subtitle: Four steps, in this order. No account, no paywall.

steps.title: Answer the 6 questions
steps.desc: Project name, mode, stack, agents, skills, MCPs. 2 minutes max.

steps.title: We customise the templates
steps.desc: The generator keeps only what you picked and customises CLAUDE.md, .mcp.json, .claude/.

steps.title: You download the zip
steps.desc: A .zip ready to unzip into your repo (greenfield) or on top of an existing repo (overlay).

steps.title: You run claude and ship
steps.desc: Suggested first prompt: /kickoff (greenfield) or /audit (overlay). Off you go.

WhatYouGet.title: What you get in the zip
WhatYouGet.subtitle: Everything is versionable, readable, modifiable. No binary files, no lock-in.

items.name: CLAUDE.md
items.desc: Claude Code instructions tailored to your stack.

items.name: README.md
items.desc: Project doc with your name, stack, and choices.

QuestionnaireSection.title: Configure your starter
QuestionnaireSection.subtitle: Six steps. No account. Generation runs server-side on Next.js — nothing is stored.

Faq.title: FAQ
Faq.subtitle: Six honest answers. If something is missing, open an issue on GitHub.

items.a: Yes. The code is MIT-licensed, the generator is open source, never any paywall.

items.q: Is my data sent to a server?
items.a: Everything runs server-side on Next.js, on the fly, in memory. Nothing is stored, nothing is sent to OpenAI or Anthropic. The form payload lives only for the duration of the HTTP request.

items.q: Can I modify the zip afterwards?
items.a: Yes — it's your repo. Modify, delete, add, force-push. Cordée.IA has no link to the project once generated.

items.q: Why Claude Code and not Cursor or Cline?
items.a: The starter is tool-agnostic — agents and skills work with any tool that reads .md files. But the .claude/ structure and hooks are optimised for Claude Code.
We don't. The repo is OSS. I consult on the side (Cordée.IA consulting). The generator is how I share what works in the field.

items.q: What if I have an existing project?
items.a: Pick "Existing project" in the questionnaire. install.sh only adds the Claude files on top of your repo, without breaking anything. Automatic backup of any existing .claude/.

validation.projectNamePattern: Letters, digits, dot, dash or underscore only.
validation.projectNameHint: Spaces and accents are replaced automatically (e.g. "My AI Project" → "my-ai-project").

step1.title: What's your project called?
step1.subtitle: Repo name, slug, or product name. You can change it later.
step1.descPlaceholder: 2 lines about what your project does.

step2.title: New project or existing project?
step2.subtitle: The mode determines whether we scaffold everything, or just drop the Claude overlay on top of your repo.
step2.overlayDesc: We only add the Claude files on top of your repo. Your code is untouched.

step3.title: Tech stack?
step3.subtitle: Optional. Used to pre-fill code conventions in CLAUDE.md.

step6.title: Which MCPs to wire?
step6.subtitle: You can always add more later in .mcp.json.

step7.title: Starting DESIGN.md?
step7.subtitle: The design system is the first thing a design agent opens.
step7.useExampleDesc: Handy to see a complete DESIGN.md in action and adapt it.
step7.emptyDesc: The skeleton without content. Fill it with your design agent.
step7.skipDesc: If your project has no UI.

step8.title: Extra files
step8.subtitle: Optional. You can always add them by hand later.

step9.title: Which licence?
step9.subtitle: The LICENSE file is added at the root. None = private code, all rights reserved.

agent_options.challenger: Devil's advocate, challenges every decision (Opus xhigh)
agent_options.pr-reviewer: Reviews a PR: diff, tests, breaking changes (Opus xhigh)
agent_options.page-writer: Owner of a Notion / markdown page (Sonnet)

extra_options.makefile: dev / build / test / lint / clean targets
extra_options.vscode-settings: ESLint format on save, Tailwind IntelliSense

AGPL-3.0.label: AGPL 3.0
AGPL-3.0.hint: Strong copyleft. Any reused mod must be OSS.
